titlestmt.titleproper: Center for Housing and Environmental Studies records53-4-1308
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-07-20 00:04:43 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Center for Housing and Environmental Studies records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Center for Housing and Environmental Studies. Director

did.abstract: Correspondence, project reports, accounts, speeches, publications, and committee records of Director Glenn H. Beyer of the Cornell Center for Housing and Environmental Studies.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

odd.head: General
odd.p: Digitized copy of "The Cornell Kitchen" can be found online here: https://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/40835

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Center for Housing and Environmental Studies records, #53-4-1308. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Cornell University Housing Research Center (later known as the Center for Housing and Environmental Studies) was new in 1952. Professor Glenn Beyer, professor of Housing and Design since 1947, was appointed Director of this center in which the School of Architecture, the College of Engineering, some units of the College of Agriculture, and the Departments of Housing and Design and Economics of the Household and Household Management in the College of Home Economics were all concerned. Two lustron houses were erected in the College of Home Economics, providing the central laboratory for interdisciplinary studies in the center, from which the Cornell Kitchen evolved.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, project reports, accounts, speeches, publications, and committee records of Director Glenn H. Beyer of the Cornell Center for Housing and Environmental Studies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes a DVD of the film "The Cornell Kitchen" that was produced in 1960 by the Reynolds Metals Company.

controlaccess.subject: Kitchens.
Housing.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Cornell University Housing Research Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Cornell Kitchen" was produced by the Reynolds Metal Company. It was written by Jerry Gammon; Technical Advisor: Professor Glenn H. Beyer; Cinematography: Vitali V. Uzoff with Assistant Cameraman: Andrew C. Fain and directed by Vitali V. Uzoff. This film outlines efforts to improve and modernize the kitchen.
The "original" film restored and maintained in the Reynolds Metals Company collection at the Virginia Historical Society.
